title: Competitor Dossier: agentic market intelligence
research_scope: Direct PM competitors plus adjacent agentic and CI products that shape expectations for ProductOS research and auto-research.

competitors.name: Productboard Spark
competitors.target_customer: Product managers and product leadership teams
competitors.positioning_summary: AI platform for product managers that spans idea framing, strategic context, and delivery readiness.
competitors.go_to_market_motion: PM team expansion and leadership adoption
competitors.pricing_signal: Enterprise SaaS with beta AI add-on expansion
competitors.ecosystem_role: PM-native system for strategy, feedback, and prioritization context.
implications: ProductOS needs PM-native market intelligence instead of generic research tooling.
Validation and loophole memory can remain a differentiator.

competitors.name: Jira Product Discovery
competitors.target_customer: B2B product teams already operating in Atlassian
competitors.positioning_summary: Discovery tooling tightly connected to Jira delivery workflows with embedded AI assistance.
competitors.ecosystem_role: Discovery and planning workflow extension inside Atlassian.
weaknesses: Strategy and market intelligence still look narrower
implications: ProductOS should differentiate above embedded drafting and issue linkage.
Manual fallback outside one ecosystem remains valuable.

competitors.name: Linear
competitors.target_customer: Product and engineering teams centered on issue flow and support intake
competitors.positioning_summary: Issue-centric workflow with structured asks, customer linkage, and support-driven input capture.
competitors.ecosystem_role: Operational intake and execution surface rather than full market-intelligence system.
agentic_features: Ask capture from Slack and support flows
implications: ProductOS should ingest asks and keep provenance intact when routing them into research.
The market expects tighter ask-to-research continuity.

competitors.name: Notion MCP
competitors.target_customer: Teams that want external AI tools to operate directly on workspace knowledge
competitors.positioning_summary: Context bridge between workspace knowledge and external AI tools.
competitors.ecosystem_role: Agent-friendly knowledge layer that can sit beneath many workflows.
agentic_features: Real-time read and write access for external agents
governance_features: Explicit connection model for AI tools
implications: ProductOS should offer retrieval-first agent interfaces before broader write paths.
PM-native state and validation remain the differentiation.

competitors.name: OpenAI Deep Research
competitors.target_customer: Knowledge workers who need multi-step research across web, files, and apps
competitors.positioning_summary: Deep research workflow with plan preview, source controls, and cited output.
competitors.ecosystem_role: General-purpose research benchmark that changes user expectations.
weaknesses: Needs product context from outside the tool
May still overgeneralize without PM-specific structure
implications: ProductOS should match the research-loop ergonomics while staying PM-native.
Traceability and decision packaging should be tighter than general-purpose AI research.

competitors.name: Glean Agentic Engine
competitors.target_customer: Enterprises that need grounded internal agents
competitors.positioning_summary: Enterprise-grounded agent platform with adaptive planning based on organizational context.
weaknesses: Not specialized to PM operating artifacts
implications: ProductOS should ground research in workspace memory before recommending decisions.
Canonical-source retrieval needs to precede auto-research.

competitors.name: Crayon
competitors.target_customer: PMM, CI, and revenue teams that need ongoing competitor coverage
competitors.positioning_summary: Organized competitive intel with automated analysis and reusable deliverables.
competitors.ecosystem_role: Continuous competitor monitoring and intel packaging system.
implications: ProductOS should add saved-search and watchlist behavior.
Competitor deltas should feed PM decisions instead of remaining isolated intel.

competitors.name: Klue
competitors.target_customer: B2B teams needing verified competitive insights across mixed sources
competitors.positioning_summary: Competitive enablement platform that mixes internal and public sources into verified intel.
implications: ProductOS should support mixed-source verification instead of a public-web-only view.
Public and internal evidence need separate freshness and trust handling.
